channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: FBI arrests Afghan man who officials say planned Election Day attack in the US
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The FBI has arrested an Afghan man who officials say was inspired by the Islamic State militant organization and was plotting an Election Day attack targeting large crowds in the U.S., the Justice Department said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Musk's X to be reinstated in Brazil after complying with Supreme Court demands
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — The Brazilian Supreme Court's Justice Alexandre de Moraes on Tuesday authorized the restoration of social media platform X´s service in Brazil, over a month after its nationwide shutdown, according to a court document that was made public. Elon Musk’s X was blocked on Aug.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Dominican Republic starts mass deportations of Haitians and expels nearly 11,000 in a week
item.description: SANTO DOMINGO, Dominican Republic (AP) — The Dominican Republic said Tuesday it has deported or repatriated nearly 11,000 Haitians in the past week, fulfilling a pledge to do so weekly as neighboring Haiti scrambles to handle the influx while besieged by gang violence and poverty.
item.dc:creator: MARTÍN ADAMES ALCÁNTARA and DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Colombian electoral authorities open investigation against President Petro's 2022 campaign
item.description: Electoral authorities in Colombia have ruled in favor of investigating financial misconduct allegations against the 2022 campaign that got President Gustavo Petro elected.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Pioneers in artificial intelligence win the Nobel Prize in physics
item.description: STOCKHOLM (AP) — Two pioneers of artificial intelligence — John Hopfield and Geoffrey Hinton — won the Nobel Prize in physics Tuesday for helping create the building blocks of machine learning that is revolutionizing the way we work and live but also creates new threats for humanity.
item.dc:creator: DANIEL NIEMANN, SETH BORENSTEIN and MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Israel defense minister cancels visit with Pentagon chief
item.description: Israel has canceled a planned Wednesday meeting between its Defense Minister Yoav Gallant and U.S. Defense Secretary Lloyd Austin, the Pentagon confirmed Tuesday. “We were just informed that Minister Gallant will be postponing his trip to Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: Netanyahu says replacement for slain Hezbollah chief was killed
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Tuesday that his forces have killed the replacement for slain Hezbollah leader Hassan Nasrallah in another blow for the Lebanese militant group.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: France’s minority government survives a no-confidence vote, 2 weeks after taking office
item.description: PARIS (AP) — France’s minority government survived a no-confidence vote on Tuesday, two weeks after taking office, getting over the first hurdle placed by left-wing lawmakers to bring down new conservative Prime Minister Michel Barnier.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Survivors of massacre in small Haitian town where 70 died point finger at government
item.description: Angry whispers have broken the heavy silence that fell over Pont-Sondé just days after a vicious gang attack left more than 70 dead.
item.dc:creator: Pierre-Richard Luxama And Dánica Coto, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Netanyahu confirms Nasrallah's replacement dead: ‘Thousands of terrorists’ killed
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu confirmed the death of the Hezbollah leader slated to replace Sayyed Hassan Nasrallah and warned the Lebanese people to oust the terrorist group or endure more war.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexico's president lays out a plan to combat cartel violence. But it looks like more of the same
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexico’s new president laid out a plan Tuesday to combat drug cartel violence, but analysts say it appears to be largely a continuation of previous policy.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hungary’s Orbán interrupted by activist during EU presidency news conference
item.description: BUDAPEST, Hungary (AP) — An activist and municipal councilor for a Hungarian opposition party disrupted a news conference in the French city of Strasbourg on Tuesday, as Prime Minister Viktor Orbán was laying out his plans for Hungary's six-month presidency of the European Union.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Biden called Netanyahu 'a bad f---ing guy': Woodward book
item.description: President Biden’s frustrations with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu over the war in Gaza have at times boiled over in profane terms, according to accounts in a new book by Pulitzer Prize-winning journalist Bob Woodward.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: MI5 spy chief says Russia and Iran are behind a 'staggering' rise in deadly plots
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Britain is facing a “staggering rise” in attempts at assassination, sabotage and other crimes on U.K. soil by Russia and Iran, as the two states recruit criminals to “do their dirty work,” the head of Britain's domestic intelligence agency said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hungary’s Orban interrupted, accused of 'selling out' country to Russia, China during EU news conference
item.description: Hungary's Prime Minister Viktor Orban was interrupted at a news conference by an activist and municipal councilor from the opposition Democratic Coalition party.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Travelers from Rwanda to be screened for Marburg
item.description: U.S. health officials are planning to screen travelers from Rwanda amid the outbreak of the Marburg virus in the country, the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) announced on Monday. The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) is set to begin scanning the week of Oct.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Suspect in Madeleine McCann disappearance case acquitted of unrelated sexual offense charges
item.description: A German court has acquitted Christian Brueckner, who is also under investigation in the disappearance of British toddler Madeleine McCann, in a trial over unrelated sexual offenses.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump campaign blasts Woodward; denies secret Putin calls
item.description: Former President Trump's campaign is rejecting accounts in journalist Bob Woodward's new book that the Republican presidential nominee has held multiple phone calls with Russian President Vladimir Putin since leaving office in January 2021.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: North Korea’s Kim Jong Un pushes goal to become nuclear powerhouse in warning to South Korea
item.description: Kim Jong Un issued his second ominous message, saying he plans to make Pyongyang a top nuclear player and expand its capabilities in face of South Korea-U.S. partnership.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: German leader Scholz's party moves fast to replace an official in a key campaign post
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — German Chancellor Olaf Scholz's party moved quickly to replace a top official who stepped down less than a year ahead of a challenging national election, presenting a well-connected left-wing lawmaker on Tuesday as his replacement in a post that is important for the campaign.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: German court acquits McCann suspect of unrelated sexual offense charges
item.description: BRAUNSCHWEIG, Germany (AP) — A German court on Tuesday acquitted a man who is also under investigation in the 2007 disappearance of British toddler Madeleine McCann in a trial on charges of unrelated sexual offenses.
item.dc:creator: FANNY BRODERSEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Water gushes through palm trees and sand dunes after rare rain in the Sahara Desert
item.description: RABAT, Morocco (AP) — A rare deluge of rainfall left blue lagoons of water amid the palm trees and sand dunes of the Sahara desert, nourishing some of its most drought-stricken regions with more water than many had seen in decades.
item.dc:creator: SAM METZ and BABA AHMED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russian court sentences a 72-year-old American to nearly 7 years in prison for fighting in Ukraine
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — A Russian court on Monday sentenced a 72-year-old American in a closed trial to nearly seven years in prison for allegedly fighting as a mercenary in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Vigils, protests around the world commemorate the anniversary of Hamas attack on Israel
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Commemorations and protests unfolded across the world on Monday to mark the anniversary of the Oct.
item.dc:creator: DAVID R. MARTIN, TASSANEE VEJPONGSA and JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tunisia's Kais Saied wins landslide reelection, entrenching his power in Arab Spring's birthplace
item.description: TUNIS, Tunisia (AP) — President Kais Saied won a landslide victory in Tunisia's election Monday, keeping his grip on power after a first term in which opponents were imprisoned and the country's institutions overhauled to give him more authority.
item.dc:creator: BOUAZZA BEN BOUAZZA and SAM METZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Intel defensive briefings surge as adversaries seek to influence congressional races
item.description: Intelligence community officials said they have given more than three times the number of defensive briefings this year to those who may be the target of foreign influence efforts as in past election cycles.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: Israeli military warns of operations on southern Lebanon's coast
item.description: Israel's military on Monday said it would soon launch operations on Lebanon’s southern coast, which would mark a further escalation of its fight against Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: Watch: Harris, Emhoff mark anniversary of Oct. 7 attacks
item.description: Vice President Harris and second gentleman Doug Emhoff on Monday marked the first anniversary of the Oct. 7, 2023, attacks by Hamas on Israel. The two were expected to plant a memorial tree at the vice president’s residence, in addition to delivering remarks to the press.
item.dc:creator: The Hill

item.title: Trump: Israel has to 'get smart' about supporting him
item.description: Former President Trump said Monday Israel has to “get smart” about supporting him and suggested Jewish people have not adequately backed him after he took several pro-Israel actions while in office.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Schumer: Oct. 7 'will go down in infamy'
item.description: Senate Majority Leader Chuck Schumer (D-N.Y.), the highest-ranking Jewish elected leader in American history, marked the first anniversary of the Oct. 7, 2023, attacks in Israel as a “day that will go down in infamy” and pledged America “will never forget.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, The Hill

item.title: Boris Johnson alleges Netanyahu may have bugged his bathroom
item.description: Israel also was accused around the same time of planting listening devices in The White House, The Telegraph also reported.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation
